explicit_text: I was born in Tunis, Tunisia in 1959, just a few years after the country gained independence. Tunis is where my family lived, and it was the place I always returned to despite my travels. It holds a special place in my heart as the city of my childhood and early education.

explicit_text: Tunisia is my homeland, where I was born and raised after it gained independence from the French colonial empire in 1956. Despite the tough economic conditions there, I always felt a strong connection to my country and family. I returned there multiple times between my travels abroad, and eventually, in 2007, I married my high school sweetheart in Tunisia before settling permanently in the United States.

explicit_text: In 1981, I moved to Paris, France on a student visa to study mechanical engineering. Paris was my first destination abroad, and I spent two years there before returning to Tunisia to complete my studies. It was an important step in my educational journey and my first real experience living outside Tunisia.

explicit_text: In 1985, I traveled to Brussels, Belgium on a tourist visa to join my friend Mohsan and find work. I stayed there for over two years, trying to improve my economic situation. Brussels was a significant chapter in my life as it was my first experience working abroad.

explicit_text: In 1987, I moved to Norway after receiving an invitation from my brother Mohamed, who lived in Oslo. I stayed there for two years and even met a Norwegian woman I planned to marry. However, the language barrier, weather, and cultural differences made it hard for me to adapt, so I returned to Tunisia in 1989.

explicit_text: I went to Canada in 1990 on a tourist visa and submitted an immigration application, hoping to start a new life there. I stayed and worked for about a year but faced setbacks when my friends, whom I trusted to handle my immigration process, betrayed me. This disappointment delayed my plans and eventually led me to move to the United States.

explicit_text: The United States became a major part of my life starting in 1991 when I first arrived in Berkeley, California. I stayed with my friend Lotfi and later moved to San Francisco, where I worked and lived for several years. After many travels back and forth, I finally made the U.S. my permanent home with my wife and two daughters.
